revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ŠĘęň«ś]]
'''Gonkan''' (ŠĘęň«ś) refers to [[government posts]] in the {{ill|Imperial Court in Kyoto|en|Imperial Court in Kyoto|qid=Q11680900|lt=Imperial Court in Kyoto|1=Imperial Court in Kyoto}} that were appointed beyond the regular number of officials. The term "Gon" (ŠĘę) means "temporary" or "acting." These positions were widely used during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, while in the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, a similar system called {{ill|Ingaikan|ja|ňôíňĄľň«ś|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ingaikan|1=Yuanwaikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55521279}} (extra officials) was implemented.
These posts included positions for central government officials below the Rank of ministers (such as {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|qid=Q1354706|lt=Daij┼Ź Daijin|1=Dainagon}} and {{ill|Ch┼źnagon|en|Ch┼źnagon|qid=Q1185382|lt=Ch┼źnagon|1=Ch┼źnagon}}) and local governors (such as {{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|qid=Q1351234|lt=Kokushi|1=Kokushi (official)}}).
== Explanation ==
During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, there were many Gonkan appointments among {{ill|Kuge|ja|Kugy┼Ź|1=Kugy┼Ź|2=ja|3=ňůČňŹ┐|1=Kugy┼Ź|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuge|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugy┼Ź|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3138036}}, to the extent that Gonkan posts were almost always filled. This system emerged because members of noble families, especially the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|qid=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara clan}}, would automatically receive [[Ranks and positions]] upon reaching a certain age, regardless of their actual capabilities. Since there were fixed numbers of official posts, extra positions as Gonkan were created to accommodate them.
When comparing a regular position, such as {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|qid=Q1354706|lt=Daij┼Ź Daijin|1=Dainagon}}, and its equivalent Gonkan title, such as {{ill|Gon Dainagon|ja|ŠĘęňĄžš┤ŹŔĘÇ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon Dainagon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ňĄžš┤ŹŔĘÇ|1=Dainagon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1354706}}, the authority and responsibilities could vary. In some cases, both held equivalent power, while in others, one post was purely honorary.
For local positions like {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|ňĄžň«░ňŞą|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no S┼Źchi|qq=}} or {{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|qid=Q1351234|lt=Kokushi|1=Kokushi (official)}}, a Gonkan appointment could sometimes act as a disguised form of exile, effectively serving as punishment. These positions often lacked real power, but Gonkan officials with actual duties also existed, making it difficult to distinguish between the two by title alone. In some cases, Gonkan positions were granted to enable the distribution of {{ill|Kuge rice fields|ja|ňůČň╗ĘšĘ▓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuge rice fields|1=K┼Źkae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11392029}} without requiring active service.
According to {{ill|Takada Tomokiyo|ja|Úźśšö░ńŞÄŠŞů|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada Tomokiyo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ň░Ćň▒▒šö░ńŞÄŠŞů|1=Oyamada Tomokiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6535591}}'s "East Asian government position Konan" (ň«śŔüĚń╗ŐŠíł), by the {{ill|Nanboku-ch┼Ź period|en|Nanboku-ch┼Ź period|qid=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-ch┼Ź period|1=Nanboku-ch┼Ź period}}, Gonkan appointments became so prevalent that regular positions such as Daij┼Ź Daijin and Ch┼źnagon were nearly nonexistent.
There were no Gonkan posts for the highest-Ranking officials, such as {{ill|Minister (government)|en|Minister (government)|qid=Q83307|lt=Daijin (Japan)|1=Minister (government)}} or {{ill|Kyou (officials)|ja|ňŹ┐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyou (officials)|1=Kyou (officials)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10910812}} of the {{ill|Two Departments and Eight Ministries|ja|ń║îň«śňůźšťü|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Two Departments and Eight Ministries|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ŠŚąŠťČŃü«ň«śňłÂ|1=structure of the ritsury┼Ź government|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8193883}} system.
In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, {{ill|samurai titles|ja|ŠşŽň«Âň«śńŻŹ|1=Buke-kani|1=Samurai Court Ranks|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai titles|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'i system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11545897}} were similar to Gonkan posts in being outside the official limits and largely ceremonial. However, samurai were formally appointed to positions like {{ill|ň«ł|ja|ň«ł|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ň«ł|1=ň«ł|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11449939}} or {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|qid=Q1133663|lt=Sangi|1=Sangi (Japan)}} rather than their Gonkan equivalents. For example, figures like {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|en|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|qid=Q1194944|lt=Tokugawa Mitsukuni|1=Tokugawa Mitsukuni}} and {{ill|Tokugawa Muneharu|en|Tokugawa Muneharu|qid=Q6636871|lt=Tokugawa Munetaka|1=Tokugawa Muneharu}} were appointed as {{ill|Gon Ch┼źnagon|ja|ŠĘęńŞşš┤ŹŔĘÇ|1=Ch┼źnagon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon Ch┼źnagon|13=User:Immanuelle/Ch┼źnagon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ńŞşš┤ŹŔĘÇ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1185382}}. Sh┼Źgun families were also appointed to higher-Ranking posts, such as {{ill|Minister (government)|en|Minister (government)|qid=Q83307|lt=Daijin|1=Minister (government)}} or generals.
During the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, numerous Gonkan posts were created as functional roles within the {{ill|Daij┼Ź-kan system|ja|ňĄ¬Šö┐ň«ś (ŠśÄŠ▓╗ŠÖéń╗ú)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daij┼Ź-kan system|1=Daj┼Źkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125768223}}.
== Examples ==
* {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|qid=Q1354706|lt=Daij┼Ź Daijin|1=Dainagon}} Ôćĺ {{ill|Gon Dainagon|ja|ŠĘęňĄžš┤ŹŔĘÇ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon Dainagon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ňĄžš┤ŹŔĘÇ|1=Dainagon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1354706}} * {{ill|Ch┼źnagon|en|Ch┼źnagon|qid=Q1185382|lt=Ch┼źnagon|1=Ch┼źnagon}} Ôćĺ {{ill|Gon Ch┼źnagon|ja|ŠĘęńŞşš┤ŹŔĘÇ|1=Ch┼źnagon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon Ch┼źnagon|13=User:Immanuelle/Ch┼źnagon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ńŞşš┤ŹŔĘÇ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1185382}} * {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|qid=Q1133663|lt=Sangi|1=Sangi (Japan)}} Ôćĺ {{ill|Gon Sangi|ja|ŠĘęňĆéŔş░|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon Sangi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ňĆéŔş░|1=Sangi (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1133663}} * {{ill|Sh┼Źnagon|en|Sh┼Źnagon|qid=Q859667|lt=Sh┼Źnagon|1=Sh┼Źnagon}} Ôćĺ {{ill|Gon Sh┼Źnagon|ja|ŠĘęň░Ĺš┤ŹŔĘÇ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon Sh┼Źnagon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ň░Ĺš┤ŹŔĘÇ|1=Sh┼Źnagon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q859667}} * {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|qid=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}} Ôćĺ Gon Daisuke (šą×šąçŠĘęňĄžňë»), Gon Sh┼Źsuke (šą×šąçŠĘęň░Ĺňë»), etc. * {{ill|Hassh┼Ź|ja|ňůźšťü|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hassh┼Ź|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ŠŚąŠťČŃü«ň«śňłÂ|1=structure of the ritsury┼Ź government|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8193883}} Ôćĺ Gon Daisuke (ŠĘęňĄžŔ╝ö), Gon Sh┼Źsuke (ŠĘęň░ĹŔ╝ö) * {{ill|Konoefu|ja|Ŕ┐ĹŔíŤň║ť|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoefu|1=Konoefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11638974}} Ôćĺ Gon Ch┼źj┼Ź (Ŕ┐ĹŔíŤŠĘęńŞşň░ć), Gon Sh┼Źj┼Ź (Ŕ┐ĹŔíŤŠĘęň░Ĺň░ć) * {{ill|Hy┼Źeifu|ja|ňůÁŔíŤň║ť|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hy┼Źeifu|1=Hy┼Źefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11393702}} Ôćĺ Gon Sa (ňůÁŔíŤŠĘęńŻÉ), Gon I (ňůÁŔíŤŠĘęň░ë), e.g., {{ill|Gon no Hy┼Źe|ja|ŠĘęňůÁŔíŤ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon no Hy┼Źe|1=Gon no Hy┼Źe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11542287}} * {{ill|Eimonfu|ja|ŔíŤÚľÇň║ť|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eimonfu|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11626338}} Ôćĺ Gon Saemon (ŔíŤÚľÇŠĘęńŻÉ), Gon Uemon (ŔíŤÚľÇŠĘęňĆ│ŔíŤÚľÇ) * {{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|qid=Q1351234|lt=Kokushi|1=Kokushi (official)}} Ôćĺ Gon Kami (ŠĘęň«ł), Gon Suke (ŠĘęń╗ő) * {{ill|S┼Źj┼Ź|ja|ňâžŠşú|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/S┼Źj┼Ź|1=S┼Źj┼Ź|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q15416595}} Ôćĺ {{ill|Gon S┼Źj┼Ź|ja|ŠĘęňâžŠşú|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon S┼Źj┼Ź|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ňâžŠşú|1=S┼Źj┼Ź|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q15416595}} * {{ill|Miyaji|ja|ň««ňĆŞ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaji|1=G┼źji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11453500}} Ôćĺ Gon Miyaji (ŠĘęň««ňĆŞ)
== Unrelated Terms ==
* {{ill|Gonsuke|ja|ŠĘęňŐę|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonsuke|1=Gonsuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11542299}} - Refers to household laborers or minor officials in the Meiji period, approximately equivalent to lower-Ranking {{ill|S┼Źnin-kan|ja|ňąĆń╗╗ň«ś|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/S┼Źnin-kan|1=s┼Źninkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11445526}}.
}}
